name: Clockwork mechanism
summary: Generates exponentially small effective couplings from O(1) fundamental couplings using a chain of N fields connected with universal nearest-neighbour couplings q. The lightest mode has an exponentially suppressed coupling to a localised endpoint, producing apparently 'finely-tuned' small numbers without small fundamental parameters. Applies to scalars, fermions, gauge bosons, and gravitons; provides a flexible framework for Higgs naturalness, axions, neutrino masses, and small mixing angles.

addresses_puzzles.note: In the continuum-clockwork limit, the framework becomes equivalent to a linear-dilaton extra dimension and offers a natural-coupling solution to the Higgs hierarchy problem distinct from supersymmetry, compositeness, or extra dimensions in the usual sense.
Small Dirac neutrino masses without Majorana sector: clockwork suppression on a clockwork chain produces y_nu ~ q^N from O(1) Yukawa couplings.

touches_benchmarks.note: Continuum clockwork with TeV-scale resonances predicts O(few %) coupling deviations testable at HL-LHC.
Clockwork-axion variants predict QCD axion with relaxed photon coupling g_a gamma, in haloscope-sensitivity windows.

new_fields: chain of N copies of a field with nearest-neighbour couplings
new_scales: clockwork lattice scale and N (chain length)
introduces.note: Three new free parameters: chain length N, lattice spacing / cutoff, q (the universal coupling parameter).

history: Choi-Kim (2014) and Kaplan-Rattazzi (2015) introduced 'clockwork axion' constructions. Giudice and McCullough (2016), 'A Clockwork Theory', JHEP 02, 036 (2017), arXiv:1610.07962, generalised to a unified framework for scalars, fermions, gauge bosons, and gravitons. Active model-building activity 2016-present.

references.citation: Giudice and McCullough, 'A Clockwork Theory', JHEP 02, 036 (2017)
Choi and Kim, 'Comment on "Aligned natural inflation in supergravity"', PLB 745, 105 (2015)
